sections.title: Starting out
Adventurer camp
Warrior

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Hello there traveller.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you camped here for?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: We're looking for Zanaris...GAH! I mean we're not here for any particular reason at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's Zanaris?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Ahahahaha! Zanaris isn't a person! It's a magical hidden city filled with treasures and rich.. uh, nothing. It's nothing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If it's hidden how are you planning to find it?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Well, we don't want to tell anyone else about that, because we don't want anyone else sharing in all that glory and treasure.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it looks to me like YOU don't know EITHER seeing as you're all just sat around here.

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Of course we know! We just haven't found which tree the stupid leprechaun's hiding in yet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leprechaun?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: GAH! I didn't mean to tell you that! Look, just forget I said anything okay?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So a leprechaun knows where Zanaris is eh?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Ye.. uh, no. No, not at all. And even if he did - which he doesn't - he DEFINITELY ISN'T hiding in some tree around here. Nope, definitely not. Honestly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the help!

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Help? What help? I didn't help! Please don't say I did, I'll get in trouble!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Well good luck with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no such thing!

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: When we've found Zanaris you'll... GAH! I mean, we're not here for any particular reason at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's Zanaris?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: I don't think we want other people competing with us to find it. Forget I said anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh well. Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think it's out here?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Don't you know of the legends that tell of the magical city, hidden in the swam... Uh, no, you're right, we're wasting our time here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know any good adventures I can go on?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Well we're on an adventure right now. Mind you, this is OUR adventure and we don't want to share it - find your own!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please tell me?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: PLEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEASE?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: NO!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think you've found a good adventure at all!

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: Hah! Adventurers of our calibre don't just hang around in forests for fun, whelp!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you camped here for?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: We're looking for Zanaris...GAH! I mean we're not here for any particular reason at all.

sections.title: Archer

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you guys hanging around here?

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: (ahem)...'Guys'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah, sorry about that. Why are you all standing around out here?

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: Well, that's really none of your business.

sections.title: Wizard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are all of you standing around here?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Hahaha you dare talk to a mighty wizard such as myself? I bet you can't even cast windstrike yet amateur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...You're an idiot.

sections.title: Monk

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are all of you standing around here?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: None of your business. Get lost.

sections.title: Talking to Warrior again after learning about Zanaris

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So let me get this straight: I need to search the trees around here for a leprechaun; and then when I find him, he will tell me where this 'Zanaris' is?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: What? How did you know that? Uh... I mean, no, no you're very wrong. Very wrong, and not right at all, and I definitely didn't tell you about that at all.

sections.title: Talking to Archer again after learning about Zanaris

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I hear theres a leprechaun around here who can show me the way to Zanaris?

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: ...W-what? How did you..?

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: No. You're wrong. Now go away.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard again after learning about Zanaris

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Found that leprechaun yet?

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Hahaha go away amateur! You're not worthy of joining our great group!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...right.

sections.title: Talking to Monk again after learning about Zanaris

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found the tree with the leprechaun yet?

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: No, we've looked for ages but haven't... Hey! Wait a minute! How did you know about that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information!

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: ...You tricked me. I'm not talking to you anymore.

sections.title: Talking to a tool leprechaun
Leprechaun in a tree
Talking to Shamus before starting the quest

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Ay yer big elephant! Yer've caught me, to be sure! What would an elephant like yer be wanting wid ol' Shamus then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Well you'll have to be catchin' me again when yer are, elephant!

sections.title: Attempting to chop the tree after Shamus has appeared

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Hey! Yer big elephant! Don't go choppin' down me house, now!

sections.title: First talk

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Ay yer big elephant! Yer've caught me, to be sure! What would an elephant like yer be wanting wid ol' Shamus then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I want to find Zanaris.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Zanaris is it now? Well well well... Yer'll be needing to be going to that funny little shed out there in the swamp, so you will.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...but... I thought... Zanaris was a city...?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Aye that it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...How does it fit in a shed then?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Ah yer stupid elephant! The city isn't IN the shed! The doorway to the shed is being a portal to Zanaris, so it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just walk into the shed and end up in Zanaris then?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Oh, was I fergetting to say? Yer need to be carrying a Dramenwood staff to be getting there! Otherwise Yer'll just be ending up in the shed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where would I get a staff?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Dramenwood staffs are crafted from branches of the Dramen tree, so they are. I hear there's a Dramen tree over on the island of Entrana in a cave

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: or some such. There would probably be a good place for an elephant like yer to be starting looking I reckon.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: The monks are running a ship from Port Sarim to Entrana, I hear too. Now leave me alone yer elephant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been in that shed. I didn't see a city.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Oh, was I fergetting to say? Yer need to be carrying a Dramenwood staff to be getting there! Otherwise Yer'll just be ending up in the shed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where would I get a staff?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Dramenwood staffs are crafted from branches of the Dramen tree, so they are. I hear there's a Dramen tree over on the island of Entrana in a cave

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: or some such. There would probably be a good place for an elephant like yer to be starting looking I reckon.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: The monks are running a ship from Port Sarim to Entrana, I hear too. Now leave me alone yer elephant!

sections.title: Subsequent talks

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Ay yer big elephant! Yer've caught me, to be sure! What would an elephant like yer be wanting wid ol' Shamus then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure.

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Ha! Look at yer! Look at the stupid elephant who tries to go catching a leprechaun when he don't even be knowing what he wants!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get to Zanaris again?

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: Yer stupid elephant! I'll tell yer again! Yer need to be entering the shed in the middle of the swamp while holding a dramenwood staff! Yer can make the Dramen staff

lines.speaker: Shamus
lines.text: from a dramen tree branch, and there's a Dramen tree on Entrana! Now leave me alone yer great elephant!

sections.title: Talking to Warrior after talking to Shamus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Warrior
lines.text: We're still searching for Zanaris...GAH! I mean we're not doing anything here at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't found it yet either.

sections.title: Talking to Archer after talking to Shamus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you didn't find the entrance to Zanaris yet, huh?

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: Don't tell me a novice like YOU has found it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yep. Found it REALLY easily too.

lines.speaker: Archer
lines.text: ...I cannot believe that someone like YOU could find the portal where experienced adventurers such as ourselves could not.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Believe what you want. Enjoy your little camp fire.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard after talking to Shamus

lines.speaker: Wizard
lines.text: Hahaha you're such an amateur! Go away and play with some cabbage amateur!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...right.

sections.title: Talking to Monk after talking to Shamus

lines.speaker: Monk
lines.text: I already told you. I'm not talking to you anymore.

lines.speaker: Tool Leprechaun
lines.text: Ah, 'tis a foine day to be sure! Were yez wantin' me to store yer tools, or maybe ye might be wantin' yer stuff back from me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me about the Lost City?

lines.speaker: Tool Leprechaun
lines.text: Nay, ye be wantin' to ask Shamus if ye wants to know about the Lost City. He be living up a tree somewhere near Draynor Village. Last I heard he were wailin' about some biggun tryin' to chop his tree down!

sections.title: The island of Entrana
Cave monk

lines.speaker: Cave monk
lines.text: Be careful going in there! You are unarmed, and there is much evilness lurking down there! The evilness seems to block off our contact with our gods,

lines.speaker: Cave monk
lines.text: so our prayers seem to have less effect down there. Oh, also, you won't be able to come back this way - This ladder only goes one way!

lines.speaker: Cave monk
lines.text: The only exit from the caves below is a portal which leads only to the deepest wilderness!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think I'm strong enough to enter then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that is a risk I will have to take.

sections.title: The Dramen tree
Attempting to chop down the tree
Attempting to chop down the tree while the Tree Spirit is still alive

lines.speaker: Tree spirit
lines.text: You must defeat me before touching the tree!

sections.title: After the Tree Spirit is defeated

lines.speaker: Tree spirit
lines.text: You must defeat me before touching the tree!
